section_title: A guarantee which extends to a series of transactions is called a
content:
129. A guarantee which extends to a series of transactions is called a
guarantee"
"continuing guarantee".
Illustrations
(a) A, in consideration that B will employ C in collecting the rent of B's
zamindari, promises B to be responsible, to the amount of 5,000 Taka, for the
due collection and payment by C of those rents. This is a continuing
guarantee.
(b) A guarantees payment to B, a tea-dealer, to the amount of ┬ú 100, for
any tea he may from time to time supply to C. B supplies C with tea to above
the value of ┬ú 100, and C pay B for it. Afterwards B supplies C with tea to
the value of ┬ú 200. C fails to pay. The guarantee given by A was a
continuing guarantee, and he is accordingly liable to B to the extent of ┬ú
100.
(c) A guarantees payment to B of the price of five sacks of flour to be
delivered by B to C and to be paid for in a month. B delivers five sacks to C.
C pays for them. Afterwards B delivers four sacks to C, which C does not pay
for. The guarantee given by A was not a continuing guarantee, and
accordingly he is not liable for the price of the four sacks.
Revocation

for. The guarantee given by A was not a continuing guarantee, and
accordingly he is not liable for the price of the four sacks.
Revocation
